headline: The Sea Is Coming and the Plan Is Not Ready
context: NAHRIM projects 0.7-metre sea level rise by 2100 affecting 2.5 million Malaysians in coastal zones, but the National Adaptation Plan remains in draft since 2022.

cards.big: 2.5 million Malaysians live in zones that will flood this century.
cards.sub: The National Adaptation Plan has been in draft for four years.

cards.big: Tanjung Piai lost 200 hectares of coastline in 20 years.
cards.sub: Southern Johor's mangrove buffer is retreating faster than any model predicted.

cards.big: Flood damage costs averaged RM6.1B annually from 2019 to 2024.
cards.sub: Insurance penetration in flood zones: under 10%. The public bears the cost.

cards.big: Penang, Johor, Sabah have no state-level adaptation budgets.
cards.sub: Climate is federal. Land is state. Infrastructure is both. Nobody owns the problem.

cards.big: Adaptation is not environmental policy. It is economic survival.
cards.sub: Every year of delay increases the eventual cost. Physics does not negotiate.

cards.big: The sea does not wait for interministerial coordination. Malaysia needs a plan with a budget, not a draft with a date.
